title: Scouting Report: Edin Dzeko Still Questionable Ahead Of Canada Tilt - Northern Tribune
original_text:
Scouting Report: Edin Dzeko Still Questionable Ahead Of Canada Tilt
With Canada set to host Bosnia and Herzegovina on Friday, both countries march towards the fixture while their star players race to get match fit in time for the tournament’s opening stanza.
In what has become a mirrored storyline, both Canada’s Alphonso Davies and Bosnia-Herzegovina’s Edin Dzeko are in the final stages of physical rehabilitation while Friday’s fixture looms ever closer. At the moment, however, whether Davies or Dzeko will ultimately be fit enough to influence the match remains highly in doubt.
While Canada’s Alphonso Davies has been on a return-to-play program as Les Rouges prepare to host Bosnia and Herzegovina in downtown Toronto, the visitors saw team captain Edin Dzeko remain limited to individual training today in Etobicoke.
The 40-year-old Edin Dzeko, who suffered a shoulder injury in Bosnia and Herzegovina’s historic win over Italy, has amassed an astounding 148 caps for his country and scored 73 goals, but has only managed 64 minutes of cumulative game since his March-time shoulder injury.
The veteran is a key player for the team, though he was forced to ride the bench throughout his country’s two World Cup qualifiers against North Macedonia and Panama, both of which ended in draws.
In fact, the Bosnian forward line is in a spell of trouble. Both Dzeko and forward Haris Tabakovic sat out of training today, with the duo receiving physical therapy before progressing to some individual work rather than team-based activities.
Defensive midfielder Ivan Sunjic has returned to team activities and is expected to be a starter against Canada on June 12.
The media could not be played.
Reload
Should the veteran Dzeko be sidelined for the Bosnian opener against Canada, Ermedin Demirovic will be stepping up to produce the goals. The Stuttgart forward, 28, has been Dzeko’s understudy since joining the national team in 2021 and has frequently partnered him up top, though his track record with the national team does not reflect his strong club form.
Meanwhile, Canada is not expecting Alphonso Davies to feature in the country’s opener – at least, that has been the party line. That would be fine with Bosnia and Herzegovina keeper Nikola Vasilj, who is based in Germany and has seen Davies up close, describing the Bayern Munich man as Canada’s most important player.
“That’s, of course, a good thing for us,” says the St. Pauli keeper on Davies’ expected absence, though he says it doesn’t impact his nation’s game plan. “It’s just about putting the focus on our game and what we can do, and just doing things that we showed we can do in the qualifications.”
With the aforementioned Haris Tabakovic still questionable as he recovers from a fractured metatarsal, the two camps have yet another mirror: it’s a case unfolding like Canada’s Moise Bombito, where it seems like the player will be given as much time to get as close to match fit as possible before a declaration of whether to keep them on the 26-man roster or name in a replacement.
Canada’s own World Cup warm-ups saw the country best Uzbekistan before finishing with a draw against Ireland, with the squad seeing Marcelo Flores replaced by left winger Jayden Nelson today. Alphonso Davies isn’t the only player on return-to-play protocol either, with Ralph Priso – another fringe replacement prospect – still on the road to full fitness, while many of Canada’s current 26-man squad having experienced their own injury issues this year.
Dzeko, who along with the 32-year-old Kolasinac was with the national team in Bosnia’s first-ever World Cup outing some twelve years ago, suggests that the end of his own playing career might be approaching. Given that, one imagines the 40-year-old will do all he can to help lead the nation through what is expected to be his last World Cup.
“Sometimes there is an end for all of us,” says Dzeko. “Maybe mine is coming soon. But not quite yet.”
Canada will kick off against Bosnia and Herzegovina on Friday, June 12 at 3:00PM EST.
